notes:
lạc = lost. You use it when someone can’t find the way, or gets separated from people.

It works like a verb or adjective: you can lạc đường (lose your way) or bị lạc (get lost). It also describes feeling out of place or disconnected in a new situation.

Very common North–South difference: in the North, lạc also means “peanut”; in the South, people usually say đậu phộng.
exampleSentences: Em bị lạc đường khi đi đến nhà bạn hôm qua.
Anh cảm thấy lạc khi đến thành phố mới lần đầu tiên.
Chị từng lạc trong siêu thị khi còn nhỏ.

translation.en: This dish has a very nutty taste.

notes_md:
**lạc** = lost. You use it when someone can’t find the way, or gets separated from people.

It works like a verb or adjective: you can *lạc đường* (lose your way) or *bị lạc* (get lost). It also describes feeling out of place or disconnected in a new situation.


Very common North–South difference: in the North, **lạc** also means “peanut”; in the South, people usually say **đậu phộng**.

definitions.vi: Bị mất hoặc không tìm thấy.

example.en: I got lost in the forest.
example.vi: Tôi đã lạc đường trong rừng.

translation.en: This dish has a very nutty taste.

exampleSentencesTranslated: I got lost on the way to my friend's house yesterday.
I felt lost when I came to a new city for the first time.
She was lost in the supermarket when she was a child.
